doc.code_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.code_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.code_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.code_best_practice_1: Use Code consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.code_best_practice_2: Test Code behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.code_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Markdown better suits the use case.
doc.code_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.code_choice_self_when: When Code functionality with standard options is required.
doc.code_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.code_design_intent: Code provides a consistent, reusable pattern for displaying inline code or code blocks, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.code_long_desc: Used to display inline code or code blocks
doc.code_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.code_note_desc:
> Code blocks in the Code component (when the block property is true) have horizontal scrolling enabled.
> This is because forcibly wrapping long lines of code breaks indentation and structure, reducing readability.
doc.code_scenario_1: Integrating Code into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.code_scenario_2: Using Code in management views for information organization.
doc.code_title: Component for displaying inline code or code blocks

doc.errorboundary_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Result better suits the use case.
doc.errorboundary_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.errorboundary_choice_self_when: When ErrorBoundary functionality with standard options is required.
doc.errorboundary_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.errorboundary_custom_desc: By providing fallback as a function, you can freely customize the error message and reset button.
doc.errorboundary_def_desc: Displays a default error UI with a "Try again" button.
doc.errorboundary_design_intent: ErrorBoundary provides a consistent, reusable pattern for catching runtime errors and displaying fallback UI, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.errorboundary_feat_1_desc: Prevents the entire app from crashing due to the failure of a single component.
doc.errorboundary_feat_2_desc: You can provide your own fallback UI or use the default one.
doc.errorboundary_feat_3_desc: Includes a reset mechanism to retry rendering of child elements.
doc.errorboundary_long_desc: The ErrorBoundary component catches JavaScript errors that occur anywhere in its child component tree, logs those errors, and displays a fallback UI instead of the crashed component tree.
doc.errorboundary_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.errorboundary_scenario_1: Integrating ErrorBoundary into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.errorboundary_scenario_2: Using ErrorBoundary in management views for information organization.
doc.errorboundary_title: Boundary element to catch runtime errors and show fallback UI

doc.focustrap_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.focustrap_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.focustrap_auto_focus_desc: Setting the autoFocus prop to true shifts focus to the first focusable element as soon as the trap becomes active.
doc.focustrap_best_practice_1: Use FocusTrap consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.focustrap_best_practice_2: Test FocusTrap behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.focustrap_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Dialog better suits the use case.
doc.focustrap_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.focustrap_choice_self_when: When FocusTrap functionality with standard options is required.
doc.focustrap_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.focustrap_design_intent: FocusTrap provides a consistent, reusable pattern for trapping keyboard focus within a specific area for accessibility, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.focustrap_feat_1: Restricts Tab key movement within the component.
doc.focustrap_feat_2: Pressing Tab at the last element returns focus to the first, and pressing Shift + Tab at the first element moves it to the last (loop).
doc.focustrap_feat_3: When the component mounts (or becomes active), it can automatically shift focus to the first focusable element inside.
doc.focustrap_feat_4: When the component unmounts, it restores focus to the previously active element.
doc.focustrap_long_desc: FocusTrap is a utility component designed to trap keyboard focus within a specific area. It is used to implement accessible UIs such as modals, dialogs, and drawers where you want to restrict user interaction to a specific range.
doc.focustrap_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.focustrap_scenario_1: Integrating FocusTrap into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.focustrap_scenario_2: Using FocusTrap in management views for information organization.
doc.focustrap_title: Utility to keep keyboard focus within a specific area
doc.highlight_long_desc: The Highlight component emphasizes specific parts of a text using the mark tag.
doc.highlight_title: Component to visually emphasize specific keywords in text

doc.qrcode_desc_2: QRCode renders a specified string as a QR code. It supports output in SVG or Canvas format and allows customization of size, color, and error correction level.
doc.qrcode_design_intent: QRCode provides a consistent, reusable pattern for generating QR codes from text or URLs, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.

doc.qrcode_var_color_desc: Use bgColor and fgColor to change the background and foreground (dot) colors.
doc.qrcode_var_logo_desc: Use imageSettings to place a logo image in the center of the QR code.
doc.qrcode_var_size_desc: You can specify the width and height of the QR code with the size prop.

doc.code_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the code block. If a copy button is present, Tab reaches it and Enter or Space copies the content.
doc.code_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop of the copy button.
doc.errorboundary_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction. Tab moves focus through any action buttons in the fallback UI.
doc.errorboundary_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the fallback content children.
doc.focustrap_keyboard_desc: Tab cycles focus through all focusable elements within the trap. Shift+Tab moves focus backward. Focus does not escape the trap boundaries while it is active.
doc.focustrap_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the FocusTrap component.
doc.kbd_keyboard_desc: This component is a display element for showing keyboard key labels. It does not require specific keyboard interaction of its own.
doc.kbd_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the children prop.
doc.qrcode_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction. Provide a meaningful aria-label describing the QR code content for screen reader accessibility.
doc.qrcode_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop.
